Oil prices stabilise as investors weigh Iran war end, Hormuz reopening uncertainty
Oil Futures Rise on Likely Technical Recovery

holdings.infoBox: GUSH is an equity ETF with a total of 64 individual holdings.

overview.description: The Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exp. & Prod. Bull 2X ETF (GUSH) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in energy equity. The fund provides 2x daily exposure to an equal-weighted index of the largest oil and gas exploration and production companies in the US. GUSH was launched on May 28, 2015 and is issued by Direxion.

data.title: Natural Gas and Oil Forecast: WTI Under $76 While Brent Tests $78 — NatGas Bullish Continuation?
data.text: With geopolitical risk premium largely removed, oil benchmarks faced renewed selling pressure while natural gas showed resilience. WTI confirms bearish breakdown targeting $68.50, Brent tests channel ...

data.title: Oil prices stabilise as investors weigh Iran war end, Hormuz reopening uncertainty
data.text: Oil prices edged higher in early trade on Wednesday, ​clawing back some of the previous session's losses, as investors assessed whether the Iran ‌war will truly end and the Strait of Hormuz reopen.

data.title: Oil Futures Rise on Likely Technical Recovery
data.text: Oil futures rose in early Asian trade on a likely technical recovery after futures fell for a fourth consecutive session on Tuesday.

data.title: Tuesday's Final Takeaways: SPCX Extends Post IPO Rally, Oil Falls & CVNA Expands
data.text: Marley Kayden talks about SpaceX (SPCX) extending its post IPO rally and continuing gains throughout the trading day. She also discusses falling oil prices and Carvana's (CVNA) expansion into a new ca...

data.title: Crude Oil Price Forecast: Breakdown Extends Toward Key Support Zone
data.text: Crude oil extends its decline after breaking key support levels, now approaching a major confluence zone near the 200-day and 200-week moving averages.

data.title: Oil Falls Below $80 For First Time in More Than Three Months
data.text: Brent oil fell below $80 a barrel for the first time in more than three months as the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosted expectations for a revival in supply, with leading Wall Street...

data.title: Natural Gas, WTI Oil, Brent Oil Forecasts – WTI Oil Dives 6% As Traders Prepare For The Flood Of Oil From The Middle East
data.text: Oil markets are under strong pressure as traders bet on rising supply from Gulf countries.

data.title: Global oil prices break below $80 for the first time since the Iran war began. Ships still aren't passing through Hormuz.
data.text: Shipping data show that only a fraction of normal tanker volumes are moving through the Strait of Hormuz.

data.title: Crude Oil Price Analysis – Oil Continues to Fall as Peace Likely
data.text: The oil markets continue to see a lot of selling pressure, as the idea of peace allowing the flow of oil providing relief to the markets.

data.title: Major Wall Street Banks Cut Oil-Price Forecasts on Faster Gulf Supply Recovery
data.text: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley cut their oil-price forecasts with Persian Gulf flows expected to recover to prewar levels sooner than anticipated.

data.title: The peace deal is in the price: Goldman Sachs lowers its oil-price target to market levels
data.text: Risks to oil price assumptions in the event of a finalized peace deal are two-sided but the Goldman Sachs report notes that the global economy adjusted very flexibly to the largest oil production shoc...

data.title: Oil Prices Fall Further as Trump Says Strait of Hormuz Will Reopen This Week
data.text: Brent crude and WTI prices were falling again as President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz could reopen as soon as Friday.

data.title: Exclusive: The U.S. is using an Iranian smuggling tactic to sneak oil out of the Gulf
data.text: The United States military has overseen scores of secretive ship-to-ship oil transfers to keep Gulf energy exports flowing, using aerial and water drones as well as helicopters in an operation to guid...

data.title: Goldman Sachs Cuts Oil-Price Forecasts on Faster Gulf Supply Recovery
data.text: The bank now said Persian Gulf exports could return to prewar levels as early as late July—about a month sooner than previously expected.

data.title: U.S. Futures Steady, Oil Edges Lower as Markets Stabilize
data.text: U.S. stock futures were flat and oil slipped in early European trade as investors awaited further details on terms of the U.S.-Iran peace deal.

data.title: Natural Gas and Oil Forecast: Truce Stability Fuels Technical Recovery — What's Next for Oil & NatGas?
data.text: One month-plus into the truce, supply-demand dynamics support constructive price action. WTI rebounds off channel floor at $80.09, Brent holds $82.41, and Natural Gas trades at $3.170 with positive mo...

data.title: Oil prices fall, tanker bosses remain cautious on resumption of Hormuz transit
data.text: Global oil prices ticked lower on Tuesday as the Iran peace agreement dominated the G7 meeting in France.

data.title: Oil Price Forecast: WTI Tests $80 as Brent Falls on US-Iran Deal
data.text: Oil prices remain under pressure after the preliminary US-Iran deal reduced part of the geopolitical risk premium, but slow shipping activity through the Strait of Hormuz keeps WTI and Brent near key ...

data.title: OIL OUTLOOK: What's next for prices after US-Iran deal?
data.text: Lipow Oil Associates president Andy Lipow discusses the impact of the U.S.-Iran deal and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz on oil prices on ‘The Claman Countdown.'

data.title: Oil Futures Rise on Likely Technical Recovery.
data.text: Oil futures rose in early Asian trade on a likely technical recovery after futures settled overnight at their lowest level since March 4.

data.title: US Oil Reserve Hits 43-Year Low as Trump Tries to Limit Iran Fallout
data.text: Bob McNally, president of Rapidan Energy Group, said that countries across the world are going to up their demand for oil to fill strategic reserves depleted by conflict in Iran and the closure of the...

data.title: Oil prices plunge to lowest levels since early March after Trump signs Iran deal
data.text: Oil prices fell to lowest levels since early March after President Donald Trump announced a preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement aimed at ending the war and reopening Hormuz.

data.title: How the Strait of Hormuz reopening could unfold if the U.S.-Iran deal is implemented
data.text: Ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz could rise to nearly 50% of prewar levels within 30 days of the U.S.-Iran deal, according to Kpler. There are an estimated 118 tankers stuck in the Persian Gulf t...
